title: Crunchy Smoky Venezuelan Wonton Empanadas
description: A romantic Venezuelan dish featuring wonton skin, smoke, and a diabetic-friendly twist, with a crunchy exterior and a tangy, slightly sweet interior, this empanada is a must try for anyone looking for a unique flavor experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the smoked chicken breast, low-fat mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, chopped onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, salt, and black pepper.
Lay a wonton skin on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the chicken mixture in the center of the skin.
Brush the edges of the skin with olive oil and fold the skin into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Repeat the process with the remaining wonton skins and chicken mixture.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with olive oil.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 12 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the empanadas hot and enjoy.
